phase_statuses.title: Dependency assembly boundary decomposition
phase_statuses.objective: split dependency graph construction into clearer bootstrap stages so global runtime setup, app dependency packaging, and tenant dependency resolution no longer live as one cross-module control point.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: `execution-log.md` records the dependency-boundary changes and passing focused dependency tests.

acceptance_criteria.text: `backend/app/dependencies.py` no longer directly owns the full lifecycle of schema preparation, runtime service construction, app dependency packaging, and tenant dependency cache resolution in one module-level control path.
global dependency bootstrap and tenant-scoped dependency resolution use explicit bounded helpers or modules, while preserving fail-fast startup behavior and current tenant/global semantics.
focused dependency tests cover the new bootstrap seams and tenant-scoped behavior against the final code state.

phase_statuses.title: Permission resolver segmentation and legacy-default isolation
phase_statuses.objective: break permission resolution into bounded resource-specific stages and move legacy default semantics into explicit policy helpers instead of keeping them embedded in the main rule accumulator.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: `execution-log.md` records the permission-policy refactor and passing focused permission/auth payload tests.

acceptance_criteria.text: permission resolution is broken into narrower resource/policy stages instead of embedding KB, chat, tool, and sub-admin scope accumulation in one large rule path.
legacy permission defaults such as missing `can_view_tools` or `can_view_kb_config` behavior are centralized in explicit helpers/policies rather than being silently embedded in the accumulator logic.
`/api/auth/me` and existing permission consumers preserve the final capability/permission contract without reintroducing silent exception swallowing or menu/backend drift.

phase_statuses.title: UserStore responsibility split
phase_statuses.objective: keep the `UserStore` public contract stable while moving credential history/lockout handling and permission-group membership persistence behind narrower internal stores or helpers.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: `execution-log.md` records the user-store split and passing focused backend user/auth regressions.

acceptance_criteria.text: `backend/services/users/store.py` no longer directly owns all credential-history, lockout-state, and permission-group relationship persistence logic inline with core user CRUD operations.
user credential and permission-group persistence behavior remains fail-fast and transactionally consistent after the split, without adding fallback branches.
focused backend tests cover the refactored user-store seams against the final code state.

phase_statuses.title: Frontend layout shell decomposition
phase_statuses.objective: reduce `Layout` into a shell composition layer by extracting unread polling, responsive sidebar behavior, and nav rendering concerns into dedicated hooks/components.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: `execution-log.md` records the layout decomposition and passing focused frontend layout regressions.

acceptance_criteria.text: `fronted/src/components/Layout.js` is reduced to shell composition responsibilities, with responsive sidebar state, inbox polling, or nav rendering extracted into bounded hooks/components.
route metadata and shared auth evaluation still govern nav visibility after the split, with no new ad hoc shell-only access rules introduced.
focused frontend layout tests pass against the final decomposed shell state.

phase_statuses.title: Legacy compatibility isolation and focused closure
phase_statuses.objective: isolate legacy password-hash handling and deprecated user group compatibility semantics to explicit, reviewable boundaries, then run focused backend/frontend regression closure.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: `execution-log.md` and `test-report.md` record the legacy-boundary isolation plus final focused regression results.

acceptance_criteria.text: legacy password-hash verification and deprecated user-group compatibility semantics are isolated to explicit helpers or compatibility modules instead of being scattered across store and auth paths.
focused backend/frontend regression commands pass against the final integrated state for dependencies, permissions, user store, and layout.
task artifacts record final implementation evidence and test evidence for every acceptance id without requiring fallback behavior or undocumented prerequisites.

goal: Refactor remaining backend dependency assembly and permission resolver hotspots without introducing fallback behavior
